edit_commands_prompt: Send commands in this format: command - Description Example: help - Show help news - Get latest news
edit_desc_prompt: Send the new description for your bot: This appears in Telegram's bot info and on the About page.
edit_desc_too_long: Description is too long (max 512 characters). Please try again.
edit_welcome_too_long: Welcome message is too long (max 4096 characters). Please try again.
effects_title: Welcome Message Effect Current: {current} Choose the animation subscribers see when they first start your bot. Effects only play in private chats.
export_upgrade_required:
Data Export Subscriber data export requires Basic plan or above.
Basic unlocks subscriber list CSV and form response downloads. Advanced adds reaction export.
help_body: Available Commands: /broadcast - Send message to all subscribers /subscribers - View subscriber list /stats - View bot statistics /menu - Create button menus /forms - Create forms /settings - Edit bot settings /help - Show this help Quick Tips: • Your subscribers only see the content YOU create • Use /settings to customize your bot • Check /stats to monitor growth • Use /broadcast to engage your audience Need more help? Contact support in the main ChatFuel bot!
reactions_empty: No reactions recorded yet. Reactions will appear here once subscribers start reacting to your broadcasts.
reactions_title: {e_analytics} Reaction Analytics Shows which emoji subscribers reacted with on your broadcasts, with a count per reaction.
reactions_upgrade_required_msg: {e_warning} Reaction analytics require Basic plan or higher.
settings_language_title: Select Bot Language Choose the language for your bot's interface:
settings_refresh_webhook_fail: Failed to refresh bot setup. Please try again or contact support.
settings_refresh_webhook_msg: Bot setup refreshed! Reaction analytics and all other features are now active. New subscriber interactions will be tracked.
setup_wizard_commands_saved: Commands saved! Step 4/4 — Bot description Send a short description (max 512 characters). Or tap Skip.
setup_wizard_done: Setup complete! Your bot is ready. Here's your admin panel:
setup_wizard_step0: Welcome to your new bot! {e_party} Let's set it up in a few quick steps. Step 1/4 — Bot language Select the language your bot will use for all menus and messages.
setup_wizard_step1:
Step 2/4 — Welcome message This is the first message your subscribers see when they start the bot. Send any message now — text, photo, video, or any media. You can use formatting, custom emoji, and macros see below
Available macros (auto-replaced per subscriber): %firstname% — User first name %lastname% — User last name %fullname% — User full name %username% — User Username %mention% — tappable mention link (first name linked to profile) %bot_name% — your bot’s name %date% — today’s date %subscribers% — total subscriber count on this bot %balance% — subscriber’s Stars balance (not available right now) %tag:vip% — if subscriber has tag vip, else {e_cancel}
Or tap Skip to use the default welcome.
setup_wizard_step2: Step 3/4 — Bot commands menu These appear in the / menu inside Telegram. Send commands in this format: command - Description Example: start - Start the bot help - Get help Or tap Skip.
setup_wizard_step3: Step 4/4 — Bot description This text appears on your bot's profile page, before the user starts it. Send a short description (max 512 characters). Or tap Skip.
setup_wizard_welcome_saved: Welcome message saved! Step 3/4 — Bot commands menu Send commands in this format: command - Description Or tap Skip.
stats_body: Bot Statistics Bot: {bot_name} Created: {created} Subscribers: • Total: {total} • Active: {active} • Inactive: {inactive} • Blocked: {blocked} • New (last 7 days): {new_7} • New (last 30 days): {new_30} Broadcasts: • Total sent: {broadcasts_total} • Messages delivered: {total_delivered} Content: • Button menus: {menus} • Forms: {forms} • Form responses: {form_responses}
subs_search_prompt_empty: {e_warning} Please send a name or username.
subs_tag_empty: {e_warning} Tag cannot be empty. Send tag text or tap Cancel.
subs_tag_reserved: {e_warning} That tag name is reserved. Please choose a different name.
subscribers_broadcast_tip: Use /broadcast to send a message to all subscribers!
welcome_macro_hint:
{e_light_bulb} Send your new welcome message. You can send text, a photo, video, document, audio, voice message, GIF, or sticker. Text and media captions support bold, italic, code and all other Telegram formatting.
Available macros (auto-replaced per subscriber): %firstname% — User first name %lastname% — User last name %fullname% — User full name %username% — User Username %mention% — tappable mention link (first name linked to profile) %bot_name% — your bot’s name %date% — today’s date %subscribers% — total subscriber count on this bot %balance% — subscriber’s Stars balance (not available right now) %tag:vip% — if subscriber has tag vip, else {e_cancel}
welcome_media_caption_too_long: Caption is too long (max 1024 characters). Please try again.
welcome_media_prompt:
Send a photo, video, document, audio, voice message, GIF or sticker to use as the welcome media.

You can add a caption (max 1024 chars) — it becomes the welcome text.

Send /cancel to abort.
welcome_msg_none: Not set — subscribers see the default greeting
welcome_msg_title:
{e_welcome_msg} Welcome Message
{e_point} The first message your subscribers see when they send /start. {e_info} If no message is set, subscribers see a blank response. You can include text, an image, a video, or any media type.
welcome_msg_unsupported_type: Unsupported message type. Please send a text, photo, video, document, audio, voice, GIF or sticker.
welcome_skip_status_off: {e_menu} Skip to Menu: OFF — subscribers see intro text first
welcome_skip_status_on: {e_menu} Skip to Menu: ON — subscribers go straight to menu
welcome_streaming_text_only_toast: ⚠️ Streaming only works with text — remove media first.
